steps.title: Use the rectangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is length times width, so multiply the given length and width.

steps.title: Use the area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is length times width.

steps.title: Substitute the dimensions
steps.content: With length $4$ and width $37$, the area expression is $4\cdot37$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The expression $4\cdot37$ matches Choice D — $4\cdot37$. Choice A — $2\left(4+37\right)$ is perimeter, Choice B — $2\left(4\cdot37\right)$ doubles the area, and Choice C — $4+37$ only adds the side lengths.
